detaileddescription.para:
This page lists errata and the handling of their workaround. Possible ways to handle erratum:
To be implemented ( <font color="red">✖</font> embed:rst:inline :red:`✖`),
Implemented in nrfx:
In driver (driver),
In nrfx HAL (HAL),
Code avoids erratum conditions (code),
Out of nrfx scope:
Implemented in MDK (MDK),
Should be implemented outside of nrfx (other),

entry.para: The HAL doesn't read from the affected registers.
The driver discards all events after a stop event occured.
The driver does uses SHUTDOWN task instead of STOP task.
The driver does not use the STOP task and ignores any additional STOPPED events.
The driver uses a separate buffer.
Related to nRF52 Series erratum 86. Spurious samples can trigger an early END event (which are discarded). The driver uses correct calibration sequence to mitigate the PAN's consequences.
Same as nRF53 Series erratum 75, nRF91 Series erratum 30
Same as nRF91 Series erratum 4. The driver uses correct initialization order.
Same as nRF53 Series erratum 65, nRF91 Series erratum 28
The driver uses a separate buffer.
Same as nRF53 Series erratum 155, nRF52 Series erratum 267
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 212, nRF91 Series erratum 28
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 183, nRF91 Series erratum 30
24 bits in 32 word mode not supported for affected chips
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 274, nRF53 Series erratum 170, nRF54H Series erratum 205, always enabled
Same as nRF54H Series erratum 115, nRF54H Series erratum 212
Same as nRF54L Series erratum 8, nRF54H Series erratum 212
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 274, nRF53 Series erratum 170, nRF54L Series erratum 7, always enabled
Same as nRF54L Series erratum 8, nRF54H Series erratum 115
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 210. The driver uses correct initialization order.
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 212, nRF53 Series erratum 65
Same as nRF52 Series erratum 183, nRF53 Series erratum 75
